description: Ready-to-use speaking frameworks for Level 0 learners. Each template provides a structure the learner fills with their own information.

templates.name: Self-Introduction
templates.when_to_use: Meeting someone new, joining a group, or as a daily speaking warm-up.
structure: 5. Work/Study: I work as ___ / I am a student.
7. Goal: I want to learn English because ___.
templates.example: Hello! My name is Ahmad. I am from Saudi Arabia. I live in Riyadh. I work as an engineer. I have two brothers and one sister. I want to learn English because I need it for my job. Nice to meet you.

progression.week_6: Add details to each item (45 seconds)
progression.week_8: 60 seconds with natural flow, no script

tips: It's okay to pause between sentences
Don't memorize — use the structure as a guide

templates.name: Describing Daily Routine
templates.when_to_use: Answering 'What do you do every day?' or 'Tell me about your day.'
structure: 1. Wake up: I wake up at ___.
3. Leave: I leave the house at ___.
4. Work/Study: I go to ___. I work/study until ___.
7. Sleep: I go to bed at ___.
templates.example: I wake up at 7 o'clock. First, I take a shower. Then I eat breakfast — usually bread and tea. I leave the house at 8. I go to work by car. I work until 5. After work, I go to the gym. In the evening, I eat dinner with my family. I go to bed at 11.
tips: Use time connectors (first, then, after that)
Use present simple (I wake, I eat, I go)

templates.name: Describing a Person
templates.when_to_use: Talking about family members, friends, or colleagues.
structure: 1. Who: This is my ___. His/Her name is ___.
4. What they do: He/She works as ___ / is a student.
templates.example: This is my mother. Her name is Fatima. She is not very tall. She is very kind and smart. She works as a nurse at a hospital. She likes cooking — she always makes delicious food. We eat dinner together every evening.

templates.name: Describing a Place
templates.when_to_use: Talking about your house, city, neighborhood, or a place you visited.
structure: 1. What/Where: This is ___. It is in ___.
3. What's inside/around: There is ___ and ___. There are ___.
4. Location details: It is next to ___. It is near ___.
5. Your opinion: I like it because ___.
templates.example: This is my house. It is in a quiet area. It is not very big but it is comfortable. There are three bedrooms and a kitchen. There is a small garden outside. It is next to a park. I like it because it is quiet. I rest here every evening.

templates.name: Expressing Simple Opinions
templates.when_to_use: When asked 'What do you think about...?' or sharing a preference.
structure: 1. State opinion: I think ___ is ___. / I like ___ because ___.
templates.example: I think English is important. First, I need it for my job. Also, I want to travel to many countries. So, I think learning English is very useful for me.

templates.name: Asking for Help / Making Requests
templates.when_to_use: When you need directions, information, or assistance.
structure: 1. Get attention: Excuse me. / Sorry, can you help me?
2. The question: Where is ___? / How do I get to ___? / Do you have ___?
3. Clarification: Can you repeat? / More slowly please?
4. Thank: Thank you very much. / Thanks a lot.
templates.example: Excuse me, can you help me? Where is the bank? ... I'm sorry, can you repeat that more slowly? ... Go straight and turn left? ... Thank you very much!
